⚔️
Maltego — Competitor (See Battlecards tab)
Maltego is an OSINT graph investigation platform and direct competitor to Orion in the law enforcement market. Not a data partner.
🏢Private Sector Discovery — Orion-Aware Questions by Industry FINANCIAL · TELCO · RETAIL · TRANSPORT · HEALTHCARE
How to use these questions: Calibrated to surface where Orion\'s 7 investigation skills (Blockchain · Threats · Identity · Infrastructure · EDD · Scams · Cross-domain pivots) and 27 data providers (Flashpoint, SpyCloud, Hudson Rock, SOCRadar, Cybersixgill, Babel Street, Vetric, Aurelius, Whisper, ComplyAdvantage, Sanctions.io, etc.) create asymmetric value vs incumbents (i2, Palantir, Maltego, Chainalysis, Splunk). Globally applicable — examples from North America, EMEA, APAC, LATAM. Localize to the prospect\'s jurisdiction. Pair pain qualification with compelling-event timing for highest conversion. Aim for 30+ minutes of discovery before any product demo.
🏦
FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS — Banks · Asset Managers · Broker-Dealers · Insurance · Crypto-native FIs
Includes global tier-1 banks (HSBC, Santander, DBS, Citibank, Standard Chartered, BBVA, ING, BNP Paribas, Mizuho, ICBC), card networks (Mastercard, Visa), payment fintechs (Stripe, Adyen, Block, Ramp), wealth managers (Schwab, Morgan Stanley, UBS), insurers, and crypto-native FIs (Coinbase, Kraken, Binance, Bitso, Bitget). Buyers: BSA/AML/Sanctions Compliance, Financial Crime Investigations, Fraud Operations, Cyber Threat Intelligence, Financial Intelligence Units, Special Investigations.
⚡ ORION FIT
Orion combines blockchain forensics (50+ chains, 285+ bridges) with multi-source OSINT (180+ social platforms via Babel Street/Vetric), credential exposure (SpyCloud, Hudson Rock), sanctions/PEP/adverse media (ComplyAdvantage + Sanctions.io), corporate intelligence (Aurelius), and threat intel (Flashpoint, SOCRadar, Cybersixgill) — letting financial-crime teams investigate fraud rings, scam rings, sanctions evasion, ransomware payments, and emerging threats end-to-end without 5 separate tools or specialist hires.
Pain & Current Investigation Process
  • Walk me through your most recent complex investigation — pig-butchering ring, BEC fraud, sanctions evasion case. From initial alert to actionable attribution: how many tools did your analysts touch, how many handoffs occurred, how many hours were burned, and where did you lose the most time?
  • When a Suspicious Activity Report involves crypto, walk me through step-by-step how you pivot from a wallet address to a real-world identity. What data sources do you query? Where do you hit dead ends and what does that cost in case-closure rates?
  • How are you currently detecting sanctions evasion via crypto — direct counterparty screening only, or are you doing multi-hop indirect exposure analysis? How confident are you that youd catch a layered laundering scheme before its too late?
  • When customer wallets appear in ransomware payment chains, how does that signal flow into transaction monitoring? Whats your SLA from detection to action? Are there cases where you found out months later?
  • How are you investigating credential-stuffing and account-takeover attacks where stolen funds were converted to crypto? Who owns the handoff between Cyber, Fraud, and AML — and where does that handoff break?
  • How do you currently assess counterparty risk on virtual asset service providers (exchanges, OTC desks, custodians) you do business with? Who validates the multi-hop exposure?
  • Tell me about a recent investigation where you needed to pivot from a compromised email or phone number to a wider threat actor profile. How long did that take and what did the analyst have to do manually?
  • How are you handling cross-border investigations where evidence spans multiple jurisdictions? Whats your evidence-sharing process with international partners?
Decision Criteria & Evaluation
  • If we could compress a complex fraud-ring investigation from days to minutes while preserving court-defensible chain-of-custody (SHA-256 timestamping, append-only audit logs, full source attribution) — what would that change in your teams case throughput and cost-per-investigation?
  • How important is cryptographic provenance on every data point in your investigation outputs? Walk me through what your evidence package looks like today and what auditors/regulators/courts demand.
  • Whats your current build-vs-buy posture on cyber + crypto investigation tooling? Are you stitching together specialist tools (e.g., Chainalysis Reactor + Maltego + Cybersixgill + Flashpoint + manual OSINT)? What does that integration tax cost annually?
  • How do you measure ROI on investigative tooling — investigations closed, cases referred for prosecution, fraud losses prevented, false-positive reduction, or analyst-hours saved? Which of those moves the needle for your CFO?
  • What is your tolerance for an investigation platform that uses generative AI / autonomous agents? What governance, explainability, and human-in-the-loop requirements would you need to see?
  • How important is flexible deployment — cloud, on-premises, air-gapped — given your data residency, sovereignty, and regulatory obligations?
  • When you evaluate new investigation tools, who has to sign off — Procurement, Risk, Privacy/DPO, InfoSec, Compliance, Legal? How long does that cycle typically take?
Stakeholders & Champions
  • Beyond your immediate team, who else touches a typical complex investigation — Cyber Threat Intel, Fraud, AML, Sanctions Screening, Financial Intelligence Unit, Legal, Law Enforcement liaison, Privacy/DPO? Where do handoffs break down today?
  • Who would be the executive sponsor for a multi-source investigation platform — Chief Compliance Officer, Chief Risk Officer, CISO, Head of Financial Crimes, Head of FIU, or General Counsel?
  • Who in your organization is most frustrated with the current state of investigations — and would champion change? Whats their personal stake?
  • Have you already evaluated competing platforms (Chainalysis Reactor, Elliptic Investigator, IBM i2 Analysts Notebook, Palantir Gotham, Maltego, ThreatConnect, Splunk SOAR)? What was the verdict and what gaps did each show?
  • Are there peer institutions in your jurisdiction who have already deployed AI-native investigation platforms? What signals from regulators or industry consortia (ISACs, FATF working groups) are you watching?
  • Who is the technical buyer who validates tool integration with your data lake, SIEM, case management, and SAR-filing systems?
  • Which executive is feeling regulatory or reputational pressure right now — Board oversight, an MRA/MRIA finding, recent enforcement risk?
Compelling Event & Timing
  • Are you facing any regulator-driven mandates this fiscal year — AML modernization (FinCEN/FATF Travel Rule, EU AMLR, UK MLRs, Singapore PSA, Hong Kong AMLO), sanctions enforcement expansion, virtual-asset-service-provider supervisory regimes (BitLicense in NY, MiCA in EU, MAS in Singapore)?
  • Has your firm been named in or adjacent to any recent enforcement action involving crypto, sanctions, or AML — consent order, MRA/MRIA, fine, regulatory letter? What remediation must be complete and by when?
  • Are you sizing FY26 / FY27 budget for AI-driven financial-crimes modernization? When does that planning lock?
  • Has there been a recent internal incident — a missed SAR, a successful fraud, a sanctions hit not caught — that has executive attention right now?
  • Are you preparing for a regulatory examination cycle in the next 12 months that will assess your investigation capabilities?
  • Is your firm undergoing a strategic transformation — digital-asset offering, new market entry, M&A — that creates new investigation requirements?
📡
TELECOMMUNICATIONS — Carriers · MVNOs · ISPs · Data Centers · Cable / Satellite
Includes mobile network operators (Vodafone, Telefónica, BT, Orange, Deutsche Telekom, Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile, NTT, KDDI, Singtel, Telkomsel, América Móvil), fixed-line carriers, ISPs, MVNOs, data centers, submarine cable operators. Buyers: Fraud Operations, Network Security, Threat Intelligence, Trust & Safety, Law-Enforcement Response Units, Brand Protection.
⚡ ORION FIT
Orion fuses Hudson Rock infostealer data + SpyCloud credential exposure + Cybersixgill / Flashpoint dark-web chatter to attribute SIM-swap rings and smishing campaigns, then pivots to Whisper IP intelligence + WHOIS XML + Aurelius corporate registries to map full infrastructure — and traces resulting illicit financial flows (crypto or traditional) in the same audit-ready workflow.
Pain & Current Investigation Process
  • How do you currently investigate SIM-swap incidents? Walk me through a typical case from subscriber report to attribution. Whats your average resolution time and how many cases do you close per month per analyst?
  • Are you seeing rises in smishing / vishing campaigns impersonating your brand? How are you mapping the threat actor infrastructure (sender domains, hosting, VOIP gateways, downstream wallets receiving stolen funds)?
  • When a customer reports their account drained via SIM-swap or social-engineering attack, how do you trace downstream laundering paths? Most carriers have zero crypto visibility — does that match your reality?
  • Walk me through a recent network-abuse investigation — credential stuffing originating from your IP space, your subscribers being part of a botnet, or DDoS-for-hire services advertised on dark-web forums. Where did you hit dead ends?
  • Whats your current process for handling law-enforcement subpoenas involving crypto-adjacent crimes touching your subscribers? How burdensome is the back-and-forth?
  • How are you tracking credential exposure of your operational staff on infostealer markets and dark-web forums — and whats your response when you detect it?
  • Tell me about international roaming fraud investigations — IRSF schemes, IPRN abuse, SS7 exploitation. How are you currently building cases for international cooperation?
  • How are you investigating wholesale fraud and bypass — SIM boxes, GSM gateways, refile operations? These tie to organized criminal networks and crypto cashouts increasingly.
Decision Criteria & Evaluation
  • If your fraud team could pivot from a single phone number → exposed credentials → corporate identity → infrastructure → wallet → cash-out exchange in under 5 minutes with audit-ready outputs — what does that unlock for your team and whats the value?
  • How much do you value being able to hand law-enforcement partners a court-ready intelligence packet versus dumping raw subscriber data?
  • Whats your appetite for consolidating tools — replacing 4-5 specialist vendor relationships with one investigation platform that handles OSINT + dark-web + identity + infrastructure + financial flows?
  • How important is integration with your existing fraud management platforms (e.g., Subex, Mobileum, Argyle) and SOC tooling (SIEM, SOAR)?
  • Whats your data residency / sovereignty requirement given subscriber privacy obligations across the jurisdictions you operate in?
  • How do you budget — opex per analyst, capex on tooling, or risk-loss-avoided? Which budget line could a unified investigation platform draw from?
  • What governance do you require for an AI-native tool — explainability, human-in-the-loop, model risk management?
Stakeholders & Champions
  • Who owns fraud P&L — Network Security, Trust & Safety, Risk Management, or a separate Financial Crimes function?
  • Is your CISO sponsoring AI-native tooling adoption this year, or is it driven from fraud ops? Where does the budget actually live?
  • Do you have a dedicated Law Enforcement Liaison or Special Investigations Unit? Are they overwhelmed by crypto-adjacent cases?
  • How does your Brand Protection team coordinate with Fraud and Cyber? Often these need to converge for impersonation investigations.
  • Who is the internal champion who has been frustrated with the status quo and would advocate for change?
  • Who from Privacy / DPO and Legal needs to bless any new investigation platform given your subscriber data?
  • How does this align with your group/parent companys tooling — are you a subsidiary expected to align with corporate standards?
Compelling Event & Timing
  • Has your carrier been featured in any recent regulatory inquiry, class action, or consumer-protection investigation related to fraud or identity theft?
  • Are you facing pressure from regulator mandates on fraud prevention (e.g., UK Online Safety Act, EU eIDAS 2.0, US FCC mandates, Brazilian LGPD enforcement)?
  • Any major infrastructure incident in the past 18 months — telecom outage, BGP hijack, network breach — where post-incident attribution was painful?
  • Are you sizing budget for fraud-prevention modernization next fiscal year? Whats the planning timeline?
  • Has executive leadership recently asked for a fraud-loss reduction target that will require new capabilities?
  • Are there industry-wide initiatives or ISAC actions youre responding to (e.g., GSMA Fraud and Security Group recommendations)?
🛍️
RETAIL & E-COMMERCE — Marketplaces · Brands · Payment Processors · Loyalty · Marketplaces
Includes global marketplaces (Amazon, eBay, Mercado Libre, Rakuten, Alibaba, Shopee, Coupang, Walmart Marketplace), brand-direct retailers, fashion/luxury, payment processors (Stripe, Adyen, Klarna, Afterpay), loyalty platforms, ticketing platforms. Buyers: Trust & Safety, Fraud Prevention, Brand Protection, IP/Counterfeit Investigations, Account Security, Marketplace Integrity.
⚡ ORION FIT
Orion maps the full lifecycle of marketplace fraud rings: Babel Street + Vetric to identify seller/social-media presence across 180+ platforms → Hudson Rock + SpyCloud for stolen credentials they use → Whisper for IP infrastructure → Chainabuse + blockchain tracing for crypto cashouts → Aurelius for shell-company structures and beneficial ownership. One investigation, end-to-end, with cryptographic provenance.
Pain & Current Investigation Process
  • How are you currently investigating marketplace seller fraud rings — where one bad actor operates 50-200 accounts using stolen identities, synthetic identities, or shell companies? How long does the investigation cycle take and whats your evidence package?
  • When you ban a fraudulent seller, how do you detect when they return under a different identity? What signals do you correlate (device fingerprints, payment patterns, language patterns, social-graph overlaps)?
  • How are you investigating gift-card-laundering schemes — fraudsters buying gift cards with stolen cards then converting via crypto exchanges, P2P platforms, or secondary marketplaces?
  • Walk me through how you trace refund-abuse and chargeback rings that use shell companies and synthetic identities at scale. How do you build the case to take action?
  • When counterfeit goods are sold on your platform and proceeds move offshore via crypto or shell companies, whats your investigation process? How does that case get to law-enforcement-actionable?
  • How are you handling account takeover investigations — credential stuffing, infostealer-based attacks, social engineering — across your customer base?
  • For luxury/IP brands — how do you investigate counterfeit ring leaders beyond the seller account, mapping back to factories, wholesalers, and money flows?
  • How are you investigating review-fraud rings that artificially boost listings — these often tie to organized actors with broader fraud operations.
Decision Criteria & Evaluation
  • If you could automatically link a fraudulent account to 180+ social media platforms the actor operates on and to corporate / financial structures behind them — would that change how you build cases and how you prioritize?
  • How do you measure ROI on Trust & Safety investments — investigations closed, fraud prevented, platform reputation, regulator confidence, or seller satisfaction?
  • Whats your current investigation cycle time from fraud signal to action? Days, weeks, months? What would 10x faster mean for your team and platform?
  • How important is auditability and evidence-grade outputs for cases that may end up in law-enforcement referral or civil litigation?
  • How does an investigation platform need to integrate with your existing fraud-prevention stack (Sift, Forter, Riskified, Signifyd, Stripe Radar) and case-management tooling?
  • How are you evaluating AI-native vs traditional rules-based approaches to fraud investigation? What governance do you need around AI?
  • Whats your data residency and privacy posture across the jurisdictions you operate in — and how does that constrain tool choice?
Stakeholders & Champions
  • Who owns Trust & Safety budget and authority — General Counsel, Chief Trust Officer, COO, CISO, or a standalone Marketplace Integrity team?
  • Do you have an internal investigations team, or are you outsourcing to firms (Kroll, BrandShield, Corsearch, Kearney)? What does that cost and whats the gap?
  • How does your fraud team coordinate with payment partners (Stripe, Adyen, PayPal, Klarna) on cross-platform actor attribution?
  • Who is the strongest internal advocate for AI-native investigation tooling — and whats their personal motivation?
  • How are you coordinating with peer marketplaces on cross-platform fraud rings (e.g., via industry working groups, ISACs)?
  • Who validates technical fit — your Data / ML team or your Trust & Safety engineering team?
  • What is the Board / executive narrative right now around marketplace integrity, especially given recent regulatory and media attention?
Compelling Event & Timing
  • Are you facing pressure from seller-protection and platform-accountability regulations (US INFORM Consumers Act, EU Digital Services Act, EU GPSR, UK Online Safety Act, India IT Rules, Australia Online Safety Act)? These mandate verifiable seller identity at scale.
  • Has there been a recent high-profile fraud incident on your platform thats now executive- or board-attention?
  • Are you scaling investigation capacity ahead of holiday / peak seasons when fraud spikes?
  • Has there been a recent law-enforcement or regulator inquiry about how your platform handles fraud, IP, or counterfeits?
  • Are you under pressure from brand owners, sellers, or investors to demonstrate stronger Trust & Safety capabilities?
  • Are you preparing for an IPO, M&A, or major financing event where Trust & Safety maturity will be diligenced?
✈️
TRANSPORTATION & LOGISTICS — Airlines · Shipping · Rail · Ports · Logistics Providers
Includes airlines (Delta, Lufthansa, Emirates, Singapore Airlines, ANA, Qantas), shipping lines (Maersk, MSC, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, COSCO), rail operators, port authorities, logistics providers (DHL, FedEx, UPS, DSV, Kuehne+Nagel), commercial space, ride-share, and freight platforms. Buyers: Cyber Threat Intelligence, Fraud, Loyalty/Miles Fraud, Cargo Theft Investigations, Supply Chain Security, Sanctions Compliance, Trade Compliance.
⚡ ORION FIT
Orion maps cargo-theft, loyalty-fraud, and sanctions-evasion rings by combining ShadowDragon SocialNet (180+ social platforms) for actor identification + Vetric for LinkedIn corporate background + Aurelius for shell-company beneficial ownership + Sanctions.io / ComplyAdvantage for vessel/entity/PEP screening + Hudson Rock / SpyCloud for credential exposure + blockchain tracing when proceeds are laundered to crypto.
Pain & Current Investigation Process
  • How are you investigating loyalty / miles fraud rings? Many now cash out stolen miles via crypto exchanges, P2P marketplaces, or gift-card brokers. Whats your visibility into the laundering chain?
  • Walk me through your last ransomware incident — operational systems, booking systems, port management. Could you trace the payment beyond the initial wallet you sent crypto to? Did you achieve attribution? What did you do with that?
  • How are you handling sanctions screening on cargo, vessels, counterparties, and shipping routes in real-time? Are you doing entity-level checks across all major sanctions regimes (OFAC, EU, UK, UN, jurisdiction-specific) plus adverse media?
  • When cargo is stolen and resold offshore, how do you trace the laundering path back to organized actors? Are you cooperating with insurers and law enforcement?
  • Are you tracking dark-web chatter about your operational systems — exposed credentials, RDP-for-sale listings affecting your fleet, ransomware groups discussing your sector?
  • How are you investigating booking / ticketing fraud — synthetic-identity bookings, credit-card fraud, then converted to gift cards or crypto?
  • For shipping — how are you investigating dark-fleet activity (AIS spoofing, flag-hopping, beneficial-owner obfuscation) related to sanctions evasion?
  • How are you handling insider threat investigations — corrupt staff facilitating cargo theft, sanctions violations, or fraud — across your global workforce?
Decision Criteria & Evaluation
  • How important is having vessel / fleet + corporate entity + sanctions + adverse media + blockchain in one workflow vs separate tools (e.g., Lloyds List Intelligence + ComplyAdvantage + Reactor + Maltego)?
  • Whats your tolerance for false positives on sanctions screening? AI-driven entity resolution can dramatically reduce manual review queues. What does that mean for your team capacity?
  • How do you measure the cost of a missed sanctions hit (regulatory fine, reputational damage) vs analyst time spent on false positives?
  • How important is multi-jurisdiction screening (OFAC, EU, UK, UN, Japan, Australia, Switzerland) in real-time?
  • Whats your appetite for continuous monitoring of counterparties (vs point-in-time onboarding screening)?
  • How does an investigation platform need to integrate with your TMS, port management systems, ERP, and trade compliance tooling?
  • What governance and audit trail do you require for sanctions-related decisions?
Stakeholders & Champions
  • Who owns sanctions and trade compliance — General Counsel, Chief Compliance Officer, Trade Compliance team, or a regional/operating-company role?
  • Do you have a Cyber Threat Intel team that works closely with Operations Security and Physical Security?
  • Who handles cargo loss investigations — internal Special Investigations, insurance carriers (Marsh, AON), or external firms?
  • How does your Group Cyber team coordinate with Operating Companies in different countries?
  • Who is the executive sponsor most exposed to recent enforcement actions or regulatory pressure in your sector?
  • How does Procurement, Vendor Management, and Risk validate new platforms? Whats the typical cycle?
  • Who in your supply-chain risk function would champion a multi-source investigation platform?
Compelling Event & Timing
  • Has there been a recent enforcement action in your sector related to sanctions violations? Shipping companies have faced major fines globally.
  • Are there upcoming geopolitical sanctions changes (Russia, Iran, North Korea, Venezuela, Belarus) that will require expanded screening capability?
  • Has your operational network experienced a ransomware-class incident in the past 18-24 months that exposed investigation gaps?
  • Are you preparing for EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) or similar supply-chain due-diligence laws?
  • Are there insurance / underwriter pressures to demonstrate stronger investigation and compliance capabilities?
  • Is your group / parent company facing executive-level pressure on cyber and compliance modernization — Board mandates, investor scrutiny?
🏥
HEALTHCARE & LIFE SCIENCES — Payers · Providers · Pharma · Medical Devices · Health Tech
Includes payers (UnitedHealth, Anthem, Cigna, Aetna, AXA, Bupa, Allianz Care), provider networks and hospital systems (HCA, Ascension, Mayo, Cleveland Clinic, NHS Trusts, Kaiser), pharma (Pfizer, J&J, Roche, Novartis, AstraZeneca, GSK), medical devices (Medtronic, GE Healthcare, Siemens Healthineers, Philips), digital health platforms. Buyers: Special Investigations Units (Payers), CISO offices, Anti-Fraud-Waste-Abuse, Compliance, Brand Protection (Pharma), Trust & Safety.
⚡ ORION FIT
Orion enables fraud-investigation-grade work: identity resolution to map provider-fraud networks (Aurelius corporate intel, Vetric LinkedIn) + dark-web monitoring for stolen patient records (SpyCloud, Cybersixgill) + Sanctions / PEP / adverse-media screening on suppliers and providers + Hudson Rock for credential exposure + blockchain tracing when bad actors cash out — all in audit-ready outputs supporting regulatory referral and litigation.
Pain & Current Investigation Process
  • How are you investigating provider-fraud rings (kickbacks, phantom services, billing schemes, controlled-substance diversion) where the network spans multiple practices, shell companies, and beneficial owners?
  • When a healthcare ransomware incident occurs, how do you investigate the downstream impact — patient records on dark web, identity-theft cycles tied to the breach, follow-on attacks on other providers?
  • How are you monitoring for credential exposure of healthcare staff and providers on dark-web markets that could enable insider attacks or social engineering?
  • For pharma — how do you investigate counterfeit-drug operations that span jurisdictions, use shell companies, and use crypto for payment? How do you support law-enforcement referral?
  • How is your team handling medical-identity theft at scale — tying back patient-data abuse to organized actors is hard without OSINT + corporate intelligence.
  • How are you investigating insurance fraud rings (staged accidents, durable medical equipment fraud, telemedicine fraud, PBM fraud) at scale?
  • For pharma / medtech IP protection — how are you investigating counterfeit goods, IP theft, gray-market diversion, and parallel imports?
  • How are you doing third-party risk and supplier-fraud investigations across your supply chain?
Decision Criteria & Evaluation
  • How critical is HIPAA / GDPR / equivalent compliant data handling in your investigation tooling? Orion supports on-premises, cloud, and air-gapped deployment.
  • Whats your audit-trail requirement for investigations that may end up in civil litigation, federal/state False-Claims-Act type proceedings, regulator referral?
  • Are you currently using i2, Palantir Foundry, IBM Watson, or other investigation platforms? Where are the gaps?
  • How do you measure the cost of fraud-loss-not-recovered versus tooling investment?
  • What governance do you require around AI in investigations — explainability, model validation, human-in-the-loop?
  • How important is multi-language and multi-jurisdiction capability for global pharma / medtech operations?
  • How does this need to integrate with your case-management, claims, EHR, and SIU platforms?
Stakeholders & Champions
  • Who runs the Special Investigations Unit — typically reports to CFO, Chief Compliance Officer, or Chief Medical Officer at payers? Who has budget?
  • Does your CISO have visibility into the fraud teams tooling, or are they siloed?
  • For pharma — is your Brand Protection team separate from Cyber and from Quality? Often these need to converge for IP-theft and counterfeit cases.
  • Who is your regulatory-affairs liaison who interfaces with health regulators, law enforcement, and customs?
  • How does Privacy / DPO / Legal validate any new investigation platform given protected health information?
  • Who is the most-frustrated executive with the status quo of investigations — and would champion change?
  • How are you coordinating with industry partners (payer-payer, pharma working groups, customs cooperation)?
Compelling Event & Timing
  • Is there a recent law-enforcement / health-regulator joint operation targeting your sector that you need to be ready for downstream consequences of?
  • Has your organization been hit by ransomware in the last 24 months? Most large health systems globally have been.
  • Are you in a regulator audit cycle for fraud-recovery program targets this fiscal year?
  • For pharma — are you facing increased counterfeit activity in specific markets or for specific high-value drugs (GLP-1 agonists, biologics, oncology)?
  • Have you had a recent data breach disclosure that requires expanded investigation capabilities for downstream impact?
  • Are there new privacy or fraud-prevention regulations in jurisdictions you operate (HIPAA modernization, EU EHDS, India DPDPA) requiring new capabilities?
🏛️Public Sector Discovery — Orion-Aware Questions by Agency Type (Global) LE · CERTs · SUB-NATIONAL · DEFENSE · IC · REGULATORS · TAX
Strategic context: 150+ law enforcement and national-security organizations rely on TRM globally — North America, EMEA, APAC, LATAM. Orion is COTS SaaS available on existing acquisition vehicles (federal in US; G-Cloud, NHS SBS, OGS in UK; equivalent national frameworks elsewhere). Use these questions to surface mission gaps where Orion replaces the analyst-in-the-middle pattern (5-tool chains: i2 + Maltego + Reactor + COTS OSINT + manual) with one AI orchestration layer. All examples below are illustrative — localize to the prospect\'s country and procurement vehicle.
🚨
LAW ENFORCEMENT — National · International · Sub-National Agencies
Includes national/federal investigative agencies (FBI, HSI, IRS-CI, USSS, DEA, ATF, ICE, US Marshals in US; NCA, MI5, City of London Police in UK; AFP in Australia; RCMP in Canada; Bundeskriminalamt in Germany; Police Nationale + Gendarmerie in France; Guardia Civil + Policía Nacional in Spain; Garda in Ireland; CARABINIERI in Italy; Brazilian Federal Police; Mexican Guardia Nacional / FGR), international bodies (INTERPOL, Europol, AMERIPOL), sub-national/state/provincial bureaus (state BCIs, state police cyber units, prefectural police, provincial constabularies), prosecutor offices and DA cyber units.
⚡ ORION FIT
Orion is purpose-built for case-building investigators: 7 autonomous skills (Blockchain · Threats · Identity · Infrastructure · EDD · Scams · Cross-domain) with cryptographic provenance on every data point. Replaces 5-tool analyst chains with one AI orchestration layer. Click any wallet flow → opens in TRM Forensics for evidentiary tracing. Used globally by national LE, prosecutor offices, and international bodies for ransomware, terror finance, scam rings, drug-trafficking finance, child exploitation, organized crime.
Pain & Current Investigation Process
  • Walk me through your last complex multi-jurisdictional case — pig butchering, ransomware, sextortion, drug-trafficking finance, organized crime. From victim/IOC to suspect attribution: how long, how many tools, how many analysts, how many international cooperation requests? Where did you lose the most time?
  • When you receive a tip with just an email, wallet, phone number, social handle, or domain, whats your standard pivot sequence? What tools in what order? What questions can you NOT answer today?
  • How are you currently doing dark-web investigations — Telegram, Discord, dark-web forums, marketplaces? Are your analysts manually scraping or using specialist tools (Cybersixgill, Flashpoint, SOCRadar, Hudson Rock, SpyCloud)?
  • How are you handling credential-exposure investigations when victims report compromised accounts (consumer-protection, BEC, identity theft)?
  • How long does it take to produce a court-ready evidence packet from initial lead — and what manual labor goes into compiling chain-of-custody?
  • How are you investigating scam call-center / pig-butchering operations when actors operate from outside your jurisdiction? How do you build cases for international cooperation?
  • Walk me through cross-domain pivot work — when an investigation needs to chain across blockchain, identity, infrastructure, and dark-web. Whats the analyst experience today?
  • For prosecutor / DA cyber units — how are you reviewing digital evidence at scale (phone records, comms, financial transactions, social media) to make charging decisions?
  • How are you investigating child-exploitation cases involving cryptocurrency payments and dark-web infrastructure?
  • How do you currently support asset seizure and forfeiture investigations involving virtual assets?
  • How are you handling sextortion and online-romance-scam investigations where the volume is overwhelming and resources are limited?
Decision Criteria & Evaluation
  • If you could compress days of analyst work into minutes while preserving evidentiary integrity (SHA-256 timestamping, append-only logs, full source attribution) — what would change about your case throughput, prosecution rate, and victim-outcome metrics?
  • How important is having OSINT + dark-web + identity + infrastructure + blockchain in one platform vs your current vendor stack?
  • What acquisition vehicles are you on (national framework agreements, G-Cloud-style frameworks, NASPO ValuePoint, GSA, SEWP, OASIS+, GovBuy)? Orion is COTS SaaS implementable in weeks under existing vehicles.
  • How much is the analyst-in-the-middle pattern costing you — paying outside firms or specialist contractors to do work AI agents can do? Whats the annual figure?
  • Whats your appetite for an AI co-investigator that scales analyst capacity 10x vs hiring more analysts? How does that math work in your budget environment?
  • How important is tradecraft-aware AI that knows when to pivot and when to stop? (Endless pivoting is a known failure mode of LLM-only tools.)
  • What are your data classification, sovereignty, and air-gap requirements? Orion supports cloud, on-premises, and air-gapped deployments.
  • How do you measure investigative success — cases prosecuted, victims supported, asset recoveries, arrests? Which metric would Orion most directly move?
Stakeholders & Champions
  • Who is the operational sponsor — Special Agent in Charge, Unit Chief, Section Chief, Deputy Director, or equivalent in your structure?
  • Who owns technical / IT acquisition? Is your CIO open to AI-native tooling on agency networks? How do they validate vendor maturity?
  • Are you coordinating with international partners (Five Eyes, INTERPOL, Europol, peer agencies) where Orions knowledge graph would let you collaborate across jurisdictions while respecting data-handling restrictions?
  • Who is the frustrated frontline-investigator advocate who would champion change because the status quo is broken?
  • How does budget authority work in your agency? Who can sign for a SaaS investigation platform at what dollar threshold? When does that authority sit with the user vs the CIO/CFO?
  • Who is the training / capability lead who would need to bless analyst training and adoption?
  • How does legal / privacy / oversight validate new investigation tools — internal General Counsel, external regulator, parliamentary or congressional oversight?
  • Who from peer agencies in your country has already adopted multi-source AI investigation platforms — and could give you a reference?
Compelling Event & Timing
  • Are there active high-profile cases driving urgent capability gaps — ransomware response, terrorism financing, child exploitation, fentanyl/synthetic-drug-trafficking finance, sanctions evasion?
  • Is your agency in a budget cycle right now (national budget, POM, multi-year fiscal plan) for cyber/digital investigation modernization?
  • Has there been a recent operational embarrassment — case lost due to slow forensics, insufficient attribution, evidence chain-of-custody challenge, statute-of-limitations expiration?
  • Are you preparing for a major international event (sporting, political, diplomatic) requiring expanded investigation capacity?
  • Are there new criminal-justice mandates in your jurisdiction requiring expanded investigative capability (digital-evidence handling laws, online-safety mandates)?
  • Has there been recent legislative or executive direction in your country to expand AI use in law enforcement?
  • Are you facing peer-agency pressure — neighboring jurisdictions have new capabilities and youre falling behind on cross-border cases?
🛡️
COMPUTER EMERGENCY RESPONSE TEAMS — National · Sectoral · Regional CERTs/CSIRTs
Includes national CERTs (CISA in US; NCSC in UK, Ireland, Netherlands, Norway, Finland, Switzerland; ANSSI in France; BSI in Germany; ACN in Italy; INCIBE in Spain; RIA in Estonia; MSB in Sweden; NSM in Norway; DSA in Cyprus; CNCS in Portugal; URSIV in Slovenia; JPCERT/CC in Japan; KISA in Korea; ASD/ACSC in Australia; CCCS in Canada; Cert.br in Brazil; Cert-In in India), sectoral ISACs / ISAOs (FS-ISAC, H-ISAC, E-ISAC, Auto-ISAC), regional CERTs (ENISA, OAS-CICTE, AfricaCERT, APCERT).
⚡ ORION FIT
Orion compresses CERT threat-triage from days to minutes — fusing OSINT (Babel Street, Vetric) + dark-web (Cybersixgill, SOCRadar, Flashpoint) + credential exposure (SpyCloud, Hudson Rock) + ransomware tracking (Ransomware.live, Overwatch) + infrastructure mapping (Whisper, WHOIS XML) + blockchain in one auditable workflow. Court-ready outputs for cross-border information-sharing and victim notification. Multi-jurisdiction-friendly with deployment options for sovereignty requirements.
Pain & Current Investigation Process
  • When a ransomware incident is reported by a critical-infrastructure operator, whats your current triage cycle from notification to attribution to IOC distribution? How many analyst-hours per incident?
  • How are you handling multi-source threat-intel fusion today? Are analysts manually correlating Flashpoint + SpyCloud + ransomware-tracker + WHOIS data + dark-web chatter?
  • How are you investigating state-sponsored campaigns targeting your critical national infrastructure (CNI)? Do you have visibility into threat-actor infrastructure end-to-end?
  • How quickly can your team produce a victim-notification packet with verified IOCs, attribution rationale, recommended actions, and audit-ready provenance?
  • How are you tracking credential exposure of national-CNI staff on infostealer markets and dark-web forums?
  • When a victim org pays a ransom in crypto, do you have visibility into the downstream laundering path for follow-on enforcement and asset-recovery cooperation with LE?
  • How are you doing threat-actor profiling — building knowledge graphs of known groups, their TTPs, infrastructure, recruitment patterns?
  • How are you supporting incident response by sector — energy, water, transport, telecom, finance, health — and adapting your investigation playbooks per sector?
  • How are you investigating supply-chain compromises when a vendor breach affects multiple constituents?
  • How are you handling incident-reporting compliance with new mandates (NIS2 in EU, CIRCIA in US, equivalent national laws)?
Decision Criteria & Evaluation
  • How important is auditability + sovereignty on every investigation step? Orion supports air-gapped and on-premises deployments + cryptographic provenance.
  • Whats your current build-vs-buy posture on AI-orchestrated investigation tooling? Many CERTs evaluate Orion as a force-multiplier vs hiring more analysts.
  • How do you measure CERT capacity — by incidents per analyst, mean-time-to-attribution, victim-notification timeliness? Orion shifts all three.
  • How important is being able to collaborate with peer CERTs across jurisdictions — sharing investigations while respecting national-data restrictions?
  • What deployment model do you require — on-premises, air-gapped, sovereign cloud, government cloud?
  • What governance and explainability do you require for AI-driven analysis going into government-grade outputs?
  • How are you funding investigation modernization — EU funds, national digital-strategy budgets, sector-specific levies?
Stakeholders & Champions
  • Who is the Director / Chief Executive of the CERT, and who runs the technical/operational division beneath them?
  • Is your CERT under a Ministry of Interior, Defense, ICT/Digital, independent regulator, or executive agency? That affects acquisition path and partner access.
  • Do you have an existing SOC platform (Splunk, Sentinel, ELK, IBM QRadar, Chronicle)? Orion complements rather than replaces — its the investigation layer above the SIEM.
  • Who is your operational champion — typically a senior incident responder or threat-intel team lead frustrated with the status quo?
  • How do you coordinate with law enforcement, intelligence services, sector regulators on investigations? Where does an investigation platform need to fit?
  • Who is your international peer network — Five Eyes, EU CSIRTs Network, FIRST, peer national CERTs? Could you reference into procurement?
  • Who validates technical procurement in your CERT — typically a technical lead reporting to the Director?
Compelling Event & Timing
  • Are you facing pressure from NIS2 (EU), CIRCIA (US), Online Safety Act (UK), Critical Infrastructure Bill (Australia), or equivalent national mandates that expand reporting and investigation requirements?
  • Has there been a recent high-impact CNI incident in your jurisdiction that exposed CERT capacity gaps?
  • Is there a national cybersecurity strategy youre executing against (EU Cyber Solidarity Act, US Presidential Cyber Strategy, UK Cyber Security Strategy, equivalent national strategies) that mandates AI adoption?
  • Are you preparing for major international events (elections, summits, sporting events) requiring expanded CERT capacity?
  • Are there recent ransomware waves hitting your sector or country that have escalated to ministerial / executive attention?
  • Are you applying for EU Digital Europe Programme funds, national digital strategy funds, or other large-grant cycles?
  • Has your CERT recently received expanded mandate (more sectors, more authority, more responsibility) without proportional resource increase?
🏛️
SUB-NATIONAL GOVERNMENT — State / Provincial / Municipal / Public Institutions
Includes US SLED (state attorneys general, state bureaus of investigation, state cyber commands, state financial regulators, state DAs, state tax authorities, public university systems), UK police forces and devolved administrations (Scotland Police, PSNI, Welsh & Scottish Govts), EU regional governments (German Länder, Spanish Comunidades, Italian Regioni), Canadian provinces, Australian states, Indian states, Brazilian states, Mexican states, prefectures (Japan), Korean metropolitan governments. Buyers: Investigations chiefs, sub-national CISOs, prosecutor cyber units, financial-services regulators (sub-national level), tax authorities (sub-national level), public-university CISOs.
⚡ ORION FIT
Orion lets a small sub-national investigation team operate at national-scale capacity: AI agents triage cases, map digital evidence (phone records, comms, financial flows, crypto), build network graphs, generate investigative summaries. Designed for sub-national investigation use cases — accelerate analysis of multi-jurisdictional criminal networks without scaling headcount or relying on national-level resources.
Pain & Current Investigation Process
  • How are your investigators currently handling large volumes of digital evidence — phone records, social media, financial transactions, comms, dark-web — across complex networks?
  • For sub-national financial regulators (e.g., NY DFS, Texas DOB, AMF Quebec, BaFin Länder offices): how are you doing supervisory analytics on digital-asset licensees and sub-national-licensed financial firms?
  • For sub-national cyber agencies (e.g., NY Cyber Command, NSW Cyber Security NSW, ÚKIB-equivalent provincial bodies): how are you tracing ransomware payments hitting state/provincial agencies, school districts, hospitals back to threat actors?
  • When investigating multi-jurisdictional criminal networks (drug trafficking, fraud, organized crime, human trafficking) where do you lose visibility?
  • How are you handling investigative backlog? Many sub-national units have it, and AI triage can compress meaningfully.
  • How are you currently handling cross-agency information sharing when a case spans local, sub-national, and national/federal partners?
  • For sub-national tax authorities (state DORs, Länder Finanzämter, provincial tax offices): how are you investigating complex evasion schemes involving virtual assets and shell companies?
  • For prosecutor cyber units at the sub-national level: how are you reviewing digital evidence at scale for charging decisions?
  • For public universities and public-sector institutions: how are you investigating cyber incidents and downstream impact when youre hit?
  • How are you investigating fraud against your government services — unemployment fraud, benefit fraud, public-program fraud — increasingly funneled through crypto?
Decision Criteria & Evaluation
  • How important is having an AI-powered triage layer that lets your lean team work at the scale of a much larger one?
  • Whats your sub-national procurement vehicle for SaaS — state-specific contracts, regional framework agreements, cooperative purchasing (NASPO ValuePoint, OMNIA Partners, equivalent cooperative purchasing globally)? Orion is available on multiple vehicles.
  • Are you replacing legacy tools (i2, Palantir Gotham, custom-built case-management systems) or adding capability you didnt have before?
  • How do you measure investigative success — cases referred for prosecution, asset recoveries, fraud-loss prevented, victim-outcomes?
  • Whats your appetite for federal/national grant programs to fund AI investigation tooling (DOJ Byrne JAG and CISA SLCGP in US; EU Internal Security Fund; equivalent national grant streams)?
  • How important is multi-language and multi-jurisdiction support for sub-national governments operating across linguistic boundaries?
  • How are you balancing pressure to demonstrate AI adoption with governance, transparency, and oversight requirements?
Stakeholders & Champions
  • Who reports up to the AG / Governor / Premier / First Minister / Commissioner? Often Chief of Investigations or Cybersecurity Director.
  • Is there a dedicated cyber unit separate from traditional crime divisions, or is investigation cross-cutting?
  • For sub-national financial regulators: who chairs the virtual-currency / fintech supervisory function, and do they have AI/automation budget authority?
  • How does your sub-national agency coordinate with national-level peers and federal partners? Where is there friction?
  • Who is the technical buyer who validates SaaS in your sub-national IT environment?
  • Are there peer sub-national governments already deploying Orion-class platforms? Could you connect to a reference?
  • How does state/provincial Auditor or Inspector General oversight validate new investigation tooling?
Compelling Event & Timing
  • Are you seeing federal/national grant programs that could fund AI investigation tooling — DOJ programs and CISA SLCGP in US, EU Internal Security Fund, Canadas equivalent, Australias national grants, etc.?
  • Is your jurisdiction facing recent ransomware attacks on government services that have exposed investigation capacity gaps?
  • Is there new sub-national legislation on cybercrime, virtual currency, online safety, or fraud prevention that mandates expanded investigative capability?
  • Has there been a recent high-profile case in your jurisdiction where slow investigation cycle time was politically embarrassing?
  • Are you preparing for elections or major political events requiring expanded investigation/cyber capacity?
  • Are you in fiscal-year planning for the next sub-national budget cycle?
🎖️
DEFENSE — National Defense Ministries · Defense IC · Defense Cyber Commands
Includes US DoD components (DIA, NCIS, AFOSI, CGIS, Army CID, Cyber Command, SOCOM, NNSA, CCMDs), allied defense ministries (UK MoD + Defence Intelligence; Australian Defence + ASD; Canadian DND + CSE; German BMVg + BND; French DGA + DGSE Defense; Japanese MoD + DIH; Korean MND + DSA; Israeli MoD + AMAN; Singapore MINDEF; Indian MoD), allied cyber commands, defense industrial base contractors (with appropriate clearance). Buyers: insider-threat investigators, supply-chain security teams, terror finance investigators, threat-actor attribution teams.
⚡ ORION FIT
Orion supports multi-user, multi-team operations across organizational boundaries — analysts from labs, plants, sites, and approved partners can contribute to and work within shared, governed knowledge graphs. Knowledge graphs capture entities, relationships, and analytic conclusions over time. Critical for multi-stakeholder operations like NNSA Genesis Mission, allied coalition work, and inter-service cooperation.
Pain & Current Investigation Process
  • How are your analysts currently doing multi-source intelligence fusion at the unclassified / coalition-shareable level? Most stitch together separate tools and lose context.
  • For insider threat investigations: how do you correlate exposure-data + corporate / personal social-media presence + financial signals + travel patterns + foreign-contact patterns?
  • How are you investigating supply-chain security threats — counterfeit electronics, foreign-influence in vendors, hostile-actor infiltration of your supplier base, foreign acquisition of dual-use tech?
  • How are you tracking terror finance and proliferation finance through crypto + traditional finance + corporate shell structures?
  • For SOCOM-type missions and special operations: how are you doing target-attribution work when the target operates across jurisdictions and uses crypto for resourcing?
  • How are you handling cross-organizational collaboration on shared targets without exposing your own investigations to coalition partners?
  • How are you doing open-source intelligence at scale for situational awareness in priority theaters?
  • How are you investigating foreign-influence operations (information warfare, election interference, disinformation campaigns) — bot networks, payment trails, infrastructure provenance?
  • How are you investigating defense-industrial-base cyber incidents across your supplier ecosystem?
  • For nuclear / WMD-specific organizations: how are you tracing proliferation financing networks?
Decision Criteria & Evaluation
  • How important is permissioned multi-tenant access to a shared knowledge graph — partners contribute data without seeing your users or queries?
  • Whats your tolerance for cloud vs on-prem deployment? Orion supports cloud, on-premises, air-gapped, and cleared environments.
  • Are you under FedRAMP / IL4 / IL5 (US), Cyber Essentials Plus + Official-Sensitive (UK), IRAP (Australia), C5 (Germany), or equivalent national accreditation requirements?
  • How do you balance the speed of commercial AI advancement with defense-grade security requirements? Orions clean-slate architecture integrates new AI capabilities in days, not months.
  • Whats your appetite for tradecraft-aware AI trained on real-world investigations (ransomware, terror finance, nation-state threats) vs general-purpose LLMs?
  • How do you evaluate auditability + provenance + reproducibility for AI-derived analysis going into all-source intelligence products?
  • Whats your budget cycle and procurement vehicle — direct award, IDIQ, OTA, classified contracting, allied national equivalents?
Stakeholders & Champions
  • Who is your investigations lead and CIO/CTO? Procurement may need both technical and operational champions.
  • Are you coordinating with peer national-security agencies on shared cases where a common investigation platform would accelerate?
  • For nuclear / WMD orgs: who runs your AI-program-office or mission-architect role?
  • For coalition operations: who validates data-sharing protocols and platform fit with allied partners?
  • Who is your Chief Data Officer / Mission Architect who validates technical fit with existing intelligence enterprise architecture?
  • How does oversight (Inspector General, Parliamentary Committee, Congressional oversight, equivalent) validate new investigation tooling?
  • Who runs tradecraft validation — typically senior intelligence analysts who must bless any AI before adoption?
  • How does this fit with existing AI initiatives in your component?
Compelling Event & Timing
  • Are you executing against presidential / ministerial directives on AI adoption in defense and national security?
  • Are there operational gaps recently surfaced in a major incident, training exercise, or recent geopolitical event?
  • Is your component sizing for FY26+ POM submissions / equivalent budget cycles for AI orchestration / OSINT / investigation modernization?
  • Are there recent geopolitical pulls (regional conflicts, near-peer competition, hybrid threats) that have exposed capability gaps?
  • Are you preparing for major exercises or operational deployments requiring expanded investigation capacity?
  • Has there been recent legislative direction on AI investment in defense and national security?
🛰️
INTELLIGENCE AGENCIES — National + Allied IC
Includes US IC components (CIA, NSA, ODNI, NGA, DIA, FBI Intel, NRO, NCTC), Five Eyes partners (GCHQ + SIS + MI5 in UK; ASIS + ASIO + ASD in Australia; CSIS + CSE in Canada; GCSB + NZSIS in New Zealand), allied IC services (BND in Germany; DGSE in France; AISE/AISI in Italy; CNI in Spain; Mossad / Shin Bet in Israel; ISI in Pakistan; PSIA / CIRO in Japan; NIS in Korea; ABIN in Brazil; SVR / FSB analogues across regional services). Use cases: target development, threat-actor profiling, illicit-finance operations, foreign-influence operations, counter-proliferation finance.
⚡ ORION FIT
Orion is built for sensitive investigation: trained on real-world tradecraft (ransomware, terror finance, nation-state). Cross-domain pivots across blockchain + identity + infrastructure + dark-web — knowing when to enrich and when to stop. Permission-aware integrations, deep multi-hop reasoning, audit-ready transparency. Click any crypto wallet → opens TRM Forensics for high-fidelity tracing. Already used by intelligence-grade customers across multiple Five Eyes and allied partners.
Pain & Current Investigation Process
  • How is your team currently doing target development on threat actors — identity resolution across 180+ social platforms, social media presence, crypto wallets, infrastructure, corporate ties?
  • How are you handling illicit-finance investigations involving crypto-laundering, sanctions evasion, proliferation financing, terrorist financing?
  • How are you investigating foreign-influence operations — bot networks, payment trails, infrastructure provenance, cross-platform campaign attribution?
  • For counter-proliferation: how do you map trade-finance + corporate-intelligence + crypto-finance for entities of concern across multiple jurisdictions?
  • How are you addressing the endless-pivoting problem — analysts chasing leads that dont converge, missing stop-conditions, generating noise rather than signal?
  • How are you handling ransomware-attribution work when an indictment or international action requires multi-source corroboration?
  • How are you investigating nation-state cyber operations — fusing technical indicators with corporate + financial + identity layers?
  • How are you doing open-source intelligence at scale across multilingual content (Russian, Chinese, Arabic, Persian, Spanish, etc.) and multiple platforms?
  • How are you mapping foreign-acquisition of sensitive technology — tracing beneficial owners, shell companies, financial flows?
  • How are you investigating hostile-foreign-actor recruitment patterns affecting your countrys population?
Decision Criteria & Evaluation
  • How important is tradecraft-aware AI that knows when to pivot and when to stop — versus an LLM that endlessly enriches and generates noise?
  • How do you evaluate the auditability + provenance + reproducibility of AI-derived analysis going into finished intelligence products?
  • Whats your appetite for an AI co-investigator that scales analyst capacity 10x vs adding cleared headcount?
  • How important is the platforms clean-slate architecture that integrates new AI capabilities in days, not months — vs legacy tools weighted down by services-heavy deployments?
  • What deployment model do you require — cleared cloud, sovereign cloud, on-premises, air-gapped, classified networks?
  • How important is multi-domain pivoting across blockchain + OSINT + dark-web + identity + infrastructure in one workflow vs separate point tools?
  • What governance, transparency, and human-in-the-loop requirements apply to AI use in your IC component?
Stakeholders & Champions
  • Who runs the component-level technical integration? Often a Chief Data Officer or Mission Architect.
  • Are there tradecraft owners who need to validate the AIs reasoning before adoption?
  • How does this fit with existing IC AI initiatives (e.g., AIM, IC IT Enterprise, allied national equivalents)?
  • Who is the operational champion in priority mission areas (Russia, China, Iran, North Korea, terrorism, transnational crime)?
  • Who validates cleared deployment and security accreditation?
  • How do peer / allied IC partners see this technology — are there reference customers in your trust circle?
  • Who is the budget authority for cross-domain investigation tooling?
Compelling Event & Timing
  • Are you executing against new NSPM / ministerial / executive direction on AI adoption in IC?
  • Are there recent operational pulls — regional conflicts, near-peer competition, terrorist incidents, hybrid threats — that have exposed capability gaps?
  • Is your component sizing for major modernization budget cycles next FY (IC IT Enterprise modernization, allied national digital strategies)?
  • Are there specific high-priority targets or campaigns driving urgent capability needs?
  • Are you preparing for major political or international events (elections, summits) requiring expanded all-source capability?
  • Has there been recent oversight scrutiny (Inspector General, oversight committee) on investigation effectiveness or analytic integration?
⚖️
REGULATORS — Financial · Securities · Banking · Healthcare · Communications · Energy
Includes financial regulators (SEC, CFTC, FINRA, OCC, FDIC in US; FCA + PRA in UK; BaFin in Germany; AMF in France; CONSOB in Italy; CNMV in Spain; AFM in Netherlands; FINMA in Switzerland; ASIC + APRA in Australia; MAS in Singapore; SFC + HKMA in Hong Kong; FSC in Korea; CSRC + NFRA in China; CVM in Brazil; CNBV in Mexico), virtual-asset supervisors (NYDFS in US; FCA + JFSA + MAS for VASP regimes), healthcare oversight (HHS-OIG in US, NHS Counter Fraud Authority in UK, regulators globally), telecom regulators (FCC in US, Ofcom in UK, BNetzA in Germany, ARCEP in France), energy regulators (FERC, NERC in US, ACER + national equivalents in EU). Use cases: market manipulation surveillance, broker-dealer/firm oversight, virtual-asset-licensee supervision, AML/CFT enforcement, healthcare fraud enforcement, energy-market integrity.
⚡ ORION FIT
Orion enables AI-powered supervisory analytics: automated entity due diligence + risk scoring + anomaly detection + agentic AI for case triage. Used globally by financial supervisors, market regulators, virtual-asset-licensee supervisors, healthcare-fraud authorities, and consumer-protection bodies — on existing acquisition vehicles wherever they operate.
Pain & Current Investigation Process
  • How is your enforcement / examinations team currently handling complex multi-source investigations? Manual stitching of trade data + identity + corporate intel + on-chain?
  • For securities / market regulators: how are you investigating market manipulation tied to crypto — pump-and-dump, wash trading, social-media-driven manipulation, cross-venue activity?
  • For virtual-asset-licensee supervisors (BitLicense, MiCA, MAS PSA, etc.): how are you doing real-time risk scoring on registrants?
  • How are you handling the investigative backlog? Many regulator units have it, and AI triage can compress meaningfully.
  • For healthcare-fraud regulators: how are you investigating provider-fraud networks spanning shell companies, kickback schemes, and crypto cashouts?
  • How are you delivering case-ready analytics to enforcement attorneys and litigation teams — what does the handoff look like today?
  • For prudential / AML supervisors: how are you doing cross-firm typology surveillance?
  • How are you investigating cyber-enabled market abuse — insider trading via hacked accounts, ransomware-driven extortion, etc.?
  • How are you handling cross-border investigations requiring cooperation with peer regulators? Whats your information-sharing process?
  • How are you doing continuous supervision vs point-in-time examination? AI orchestration enables continuous monitoring at scale.
Decision Criteria & Evaluation
  • How important is auditable, repeatable analytics that can stand up in administrative or judicial proceedings?
  • Are you currently leveraging existing acquisition vehicles (federal frameworks in US; G-Cloud in UK; equivalent national procurement frameworks)? Orion is available on multiple.
  • How do you measure enforcement-action throughput per analyst, and what would 10x faster mean to your supervisory mission?
  • What governance and explainability do you require for AI-driven supervisory analytics?
  • How do you balance data residency / sovereignty / privacy obligations with operational needs?
  • How important is integration with your existing supervisory technology (RegTech / SupTech stack, case management)?
  • Whats your appetite for continuous-supervision capability vs point-in-time examination?
Stakeholders & Champions
  • Who runs the enforcement / exams division, and who runs the data-and-analytics function? Both need to align.
  • Is there a Chief Information Officer or Chief Data Officer with budget authority on supervisory technology?
  • Are you coordinating with peer regulators domestically or internationally on similar capabilities? Could you reference into procurement?
  • Who is the operational champion who has been frustrated with the status quo and would advocate for change?
  • How does parliamentary / congressional / oversight-board scrutiny validate your supervisory tooling?
  • Who validates privacy / data-protection / DPO approval for new analytic platforms given the data you investigate?
  • How does peer-regulator pressure influence your tooling roadmap (e.g., FATF, IOSCO, BCBS, equivalent international standard-setters)?
Compelling Event & Timing
  • Are there recent enforcement priorities (crypto, AI-driven fraud, market integrity, ESG fraud) requiring expanded supervisory capabilities?
  • Are you executing against budget justifications aligned to digital / AI strategies at your government level?
  • Has there been a recent high-profile enforcement matter where investigation cycle time was a bottleneck?
  • Are there new regulatory regimes coming online (MiCA EU, FCA crypto regime UK, MAS PSA, similar) requiring expanded supervisory capability?
  • Are you preparing for international standard-setting commitments (FATF mutual evaluations, IOSCO assessments)?
  • Has there been recent legislative direction in your jurisdiction expanding supervisory authority?
💰
TAX AUTHORITIES — National · Sub-National · International
Includes IRS-CI in US; HMRC + Cyber Crime Unit in UK; CRA in Canada; ATO in Australia; ITA in Israel; BZSt + Steuerfahndung in Germany; DGFiP in France; AdE in Italy; AEAT in Spain; NTA in Japan; NTS in Korea; STA in China; SAT in Mexico; Receita Federal in Brazil; ZIMRA in Zimbabwe; KRA in Kenya; SARS in South Africa; OECD JITSIC; sub-national tax authorities (US state DORs, German Länder Finanzämter, provincial / cantonal tax authorities, etc.). Use cases: virtual-asset-tax evasion, undeclared foreign assets, complex evasion schemes, fraud rings.
⚡ ORION FIT
Orion enables tax-investigation teams to identify wallet clusters tied to undeclared income, link them to identity (180+ social platforms) + corporate structures (Aurelius), screen against sanctions/PEPs, and trace cashouts across 50+ chains. Demos consistently show value in the first 2 minutes — applies to tax authorities globally.
Pain & Current Investigation Process
  • How are you currently investigating virtual-asset tax evasion — undeclared wallet activity, undeclared exchange accounts, complex offshore structures?
  • For Operation Hidden Treasure-style work: how do you go from a wallet → real-world identity → tax filing reconciliation?
  • How are you handling complex evasion schemes involving shell companies, beneficial-ownership obfuscation, trust structures, and crypto cashouts?
  • How are you tracing BEC / payroll-fraud / refund-fraud rings where stolen tax refunds were laundered through crypto or layered through shell entities?
  • When you receive intelligence from FIU / international partners / OECD JITSIC, how do you operationalize it quickly?
  • How are you handling cross-border tax-evasion cases requiring coordination with international tax authorities?
  • How are you investigating tax-credit fraud rings — R&D credit fraud, employee retention credit fraud, energy credit fraud?
  • How are you doing large-scale identity-theft tax-fraud investigations where stolen identities are filed at scale?
  • How are you investigating high-net-worth individuals with complex financial structures and crypto exposure?
  • How are you handling data-driven case selection — letting AI surface high-likelihood non-compliance vs reactive complaints/leads?
Decision Criteria & Evaluation
  • How important is court-ready evidentiary chain-of-custody for tax-prosecution cases?
  • Whats your current toolkit — Reactor, Chainalysis Investigator, Elliptic, manual OSINT, SAS analytics? Where are the gaps?
  • Are you on existing acquisition vehicles in your country? What does it take to get Orion onto them?
  • How important is multi-jurisdiction support for taxonomies, languages, currencies, and chain coverage?
  • What governance and audit trail do you require for AI-driven case selection?
  • How do you measure ROI — tax-recovered, cases-closed-with-prosecution, voluntary-compliance-induced, deterrence effect?
  • What deployment model do you require — government cloud, on-premises, air-gapped?
Stakeholders & Champions
  • Who runs cyber / digital investigations within your tax authority — typically reports to Chief of Investigations or Commissioner?
  • Is there a dedicated virtual-asset / crypto unit, or is it integrated into traditional financial-crimes investigation?
  • Do you have a tech-acquisition liaison who handles SaaS evaluation?
  • Who is your operational champion — typically a frustrated case agent or audit team lead facing volume they cant handle?
  • How do you coordinate with peer tax authorities internationally — OECD JITSIC, JCTC, similar bilateral cooperation?
  • Who validates privacy / data-protection approvals for new investigation tooling given taxpayer-data sensitivity?
  • Who is the budget authority for cyber/digital investigation modernization?
Compelling Event & Timing
  • Are you executing against new tax-enforcement initiatives on digital assets (1099-DA reporting in US, DAC8 in EU, equivalent reporting globally)?
  • Is there a recent operational priority — high-profile evasion case, peer-authority pressure, public political attention on crypto tax compliance?
  • Is your authority sizing FY26+ budget for AI-driven investigation modernization?
  • Are you preparing for OECD CRS / DAC reporting cycles requiring expanded analytical capability?
  • Has there been recent legislation in your jurisdiction expanding tax-authority enforcement powers in the digital domain?
  • Are you under pressure to demonstrate tax-gap reduction from political leadership / Treasury / Ministry of Finance?

Vertical Demo Guides — Lead with Cyber/OSINT, Chain to Crypto Only When the Case Warrants
🚨
Law Enforcement / Investigators
i2 + Maltego + Reactor displacement · 5-tool chain to one platform
Open with: "Walk me through your last complex case — pig-butchering ring, ransomware, BEC, drug-finance. How many tools did your analysts touch?"
Run (Cyber-first): Start with leaked corporate credentials on RussianMarket → pivot to seller identity (Babel Street, 180+ platforms) → C2 infrastructure (Whisper, WHOIS) → corporate shell (Aurelius) → wallet cluster ($4.2M traced via Tornado → Garantex) → Cambodia compound. All 7 skills, <60 seconds.
Ask: "How long would that take across i2 + Maltego + Reactor + your manual OSINT?"
Show: Court-ready investigation packet with cryptographic provenance — 47 entities, 89 relationships.
Close: "30-day POC on a real backlog case with measurable time-to-attribution KPI."
Power stat: 150+ LE/NS organizations globally. FBI/HSI/IRS-CI, NCA UK, AFP, RCMP, BKA, Police Nationale, INTERPOL, Europol.
🏦
Financial Services CISO / FinCrime / CTI
Vendor consolidation · Multi-source fusion
Open with: "How many specialist intel/investigation tools does your CTI + Fraud + AML stack use today?" Common answer: 5-10 (Recorded Future + Cybersixgill + SpyCloud + Reactor + ComplyAdvantage + RegTech).
Run (Cyber-first): Starting from a credential-stuffing alert: SpyCloud + Hudson Rock for stolen creds → Babel Street for actor identity → Whisper for C2 infrastructure → Aurelius for shell-company structure → ComplyAdvantage/Sanctions.io for screening → blockchain only IF crypto cashout. One platform, audit-ready.
Ask: "What does your false-positive rate cost in analyst-hours? What's a missed sanctions hit cost?"
Show: SAR-ready narrative + chain-of-custody + integration with their case management.
Close: "60-day POC against 3 real complex investigations with named cycle-time KPIs."
Power stat: Used by HSBC, Santander, DBS, BNP Paribas, Deutsche Bank, Mastercard, Coinbase. Replaces 4-5 specialist tools.
🛡️
Enterprise CISO / CTI / Trust & Safety
Telco · Retail · Healthcare · Energy · Tech
Open with: "When SIM-swap victims report account takeovers / when marketplace sellers operate fraud rings / when ransomware hits a partner — how does your team go from alert to attribution?"
Run (Cyber + OSINT): Phone number → exposed credentials (SpyCloud) → operator identity across social platforms (Babel Street, Vetric) → corporate shell behind it (Aurelius) → infrastructure (Whisper) → optional blockchain pivot for monetary flow. End-to-end, <5 minutes.
Ask: "What does that investigation cost you today in analyst-hours and tools?"
Show: Court/regulator-ready intelligence packet for partners (LE liaison, insurance carriers).
Close: "60-day POC on real fraud rings — measure cycle-time + alert quality."
Power stat: Vodafone, BT, Verizon, Telstra, Singtel · Amazon, Mercado Libre, Alibaba · UnitedHealth, NHS · Maersk, Delta. Replaces Recorded Future + dark-web feeds + manual OSINT.
🎖️
National Security / Intelligence / Defense
Tradecraft-aware AI · Cleared deployment
Open with: "How is your team currently doing target development across 180+ social platforms + dark web + corporate intelligence + cyber + financial layers?"
Run (Tradecraft-aware): DPRK target → fake LinkedIn recruiter persona (Vetric) → developer identity across 12 platforms (Babel Street) → corporate shell (Aurelius) → C2 infrastructure (Whisper) → wallet cluster (TRM) → cashout via Huione. Knows when to pivot, knows when to stop.
Ask: "How long does that take across your specialist toolchain today? What's the analyst-in-the-middle tax?"
Show: Permission-aware multi-tenant access · cleared-deployment options (FedRAMP, IRAP, C5, Cyber Essentials Plus) · clean-slate architecture integrating new AI in days.
Close: "90-day pilot under existing acquisition vehicle — named mission KPIs."
Power stat: Five Eyes deployments. Aligns to Cyber Strategy Pillars 3 & 5. Used across DoD components, allied IC services, NCSC, ANSSI, BSI.
